text:
It was on a foggy night that young Sarah found herself walking through her quiet neighborhood, completely engrossed in her thoughts. She didn't even realize how late it was until a car horn startled her awake. Startled, she blinked as the headlights illuminated her path—a black cat darted across her path.

Confused, Sarah muttered something about cursed luck and swung her purse open. In it was a small, ancient-looking bell. The cat crossed the road and stopped by the hollowooded doorway of her favorite old bookstore. It looked up, its nose twitching, and seemed to wink before disappearing down the lane.

That night nothing seemed out of the ordinary. Sarah was back at home by moonlight reading a book by the lamp. But then she noticed the bell she had retrieved earlier. She took it out again and held it up.

Nothing happened...but then, with a soft tinkling, it started ringing on its own. There was no breeze to set it still—and there
